name: KBGAN
description: KBGAN is an adversarial learning framework designed to improve the performance of various existing knowledge graph embedding models. Addressing the challenge of generating useful negative training examples—a task difficult because standard methods often produce negatives easily distinguishable from positive facts—KBGAN utilizes a mechanism inspired by Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). In this framework, one knowledge graph embedding model acts as the negative sample generator to assist in the training of another desired model, which functions as the discriminator. This design makes KBGAN independent of the specific forms of its generator and discriminator, allowing it to use a wide variety of building block models. Experimentally, KBGAN was used to adversarially train translation-based models (TRANSE and TRANSD) using probability-based models (DISTMULT and COMPLEX). The framework was evaluated on the link prediction task across datasets including FB15k-237, WN18, and WN18RR, demonstrating that adversarial training substantially improves the target embedding models' performance.
